[[JA:仁壁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11376337}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Nikabe Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nikabe-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}{{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

[[JA:仁壁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11376337}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Nikabe Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nikabe-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}{{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

[[JA:仁壁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11376337}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Nikabe Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nikabe-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}{{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}}

Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

[[JA:仁壁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11376337}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Nikabe Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nikabe-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}{{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

{{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]]

former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 27|Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{jalink|Yamaguchi|Q207876}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, the {{jalink|Sannomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking shrine}} of {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{jalink|Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{jalink|Kamimusubi||Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{jalink|Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{jalink|1104|Q12490}} ({{jalink|Choji|Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|858|Q30371}} ({{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}, {{jalink|Yoshiki District|Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{jalink|939|Q23422}} ({{jalink|Tenkei|Q1327340}} 2), {{jalink|1081|Q19450}} ({{jalink|Eiho|Q1193881}} 1), {{jalink|1180|Q19702}} ({{jalink|Jisho|Q1194671}} 4), and {{jalink|1187|Q19731}} ({{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{jalink|Genryaku|Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{jalink|1195|Q19745}} ({{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{jalink|Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{jalink|Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{jalink|Kyushu|Q13987}} in {{jalink|1494|Q6693}} ({{jalink|Meio|Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{jalink|1569|Q6653}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 12), during {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567}}, when {{jalink|Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{jalink|Mori Terumoto|Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{jalink|1712|Q6871}} ({{jalink|Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 5) by {{jalink|Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In {{jalink|1997|Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{jalink|2001|Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{jalink|2010|Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 2 ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9 ({{jalink|867|Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West) {{jalink|Yamaguchi Line|Q851226}}, {{jalink|Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463}} or {{jalink|Miyano Station|Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
