[[JA:仁壁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11376337}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Nikabe Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nikabe-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}{{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|lt=Kamimusubi||WD=|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||WD=|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|lt=Kamimusubi||WD=|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||WD=|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)||WD=|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:仁壁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11376337}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Nikabe Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nikabe-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}{{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}{{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}}

Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}} Sannomiya
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:周防国|社3]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:住吉神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
