Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village|1=Miyano}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490|1=1104}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870|1=Chōji}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583|1=Yoshiki District, Yamaguchi}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422|1=939}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702|1=1180}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671|1=Jishō}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731|1=1187}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250|1=Genryaku}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745|1=1195}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270|1=Ōuchi Yoshioki}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693|1=1494}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567|1=Ouchi Teruhiro Rebellion}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566|1=Ōuchi Teruhiro}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657|1=Mōri Terumoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584|1=Mōri Yoshimoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988|1=2001}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226|1=Yamaguchi Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463|1=Kami-Yamaguchi Station}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378|1=Miyano Station}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi (city)]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956|1=October 27}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876|1=Yamaguchi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami|1=Kamimusubi}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270|1=Ōuchi Yoshioki}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693|1=1494}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567|1=Ouchi Teruhiro Rebellion}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566|1=Ōuchi Teruhiro}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657|1=Mōri Terumoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584|1=Mōri Yoshimoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988|1=2001}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226|1=Yamaguchi Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463|1=Kami-Yamaguchi Station}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378|1=Miyano Station}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi (city)]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]]

{{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} Sannomiya
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956|1=October 27}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876|1=Yamaguchi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami|1=Kamimusubi}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village|1=Miyano}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490|1=1104}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870|1=Chōji}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583|1=Yoshiki District, Yamaguchi}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422|1=939}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702|1=1180}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671|1=Jishō}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731|1=1187}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250|1=Genryaku}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745|1=1195}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270|1=Ōuchi Yoshioki}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693|1=1494}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567|1=Ouchi Teruhiro Rebellion}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566|1=Ōuchi Teruhiro}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657|1=Mōri Terumoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584|1=Mōri Yoshimoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988|1=2001}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226|1=Yamaguchi Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463|1=Kami-Yamaguchi Station}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378|1=Miyano Station}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi (city)]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]

Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876|1=Yamaguchi (city)}} 2-6-22 Sannomiya | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} Sannomiya
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 27|Q2956|WD=Q2956|1=October 27}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Nikabe Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nikabe-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Nikabe Shrine''' (にかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi|Q207876|WD=Q207876|1=Yamaguchi (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|WD=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking shrine|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsPrecinct information board.. ; Main enshrined deities :* Right hall: {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shitateruhime}} (したてるひめのみこと) :* Center hall: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (うわつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (なかつつのおのみこと), {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (そこつつのおのみこと) :* Left hall: {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|WD=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} (あじすきたかひこねのみこと) ; Associated deities :* Zuishu Hall: {{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-kami|1=Kamimusubi}} (かみむすびのかみ), plus seven others In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), only one seat is recorded. Regarding the original deity, one theory holds it was Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}, while ''Nikabe Jinja Chinza-ko'' treats Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto as main deity, Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto as co-enshrined deity, and the Sumiyoshi triad as later combined deities. === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} set shrine estate households in year 7 of his reign, the shrine was also granted Miyano-shō as shrine households. Originally it stood in {{ill|lt=Miyano Village (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q11455324|WD=Q11455324|lt=Miyano-shimo Village|1=Miyano}}, but after a strange phenomenon of jewel-like light flying all day at the current site, it was moved here in {{ill|lt=1104|Q12490|WD=Q12490|1=1104}} ({{ill|lt=Choji|Q1150870|WD=Q1150870|1=Chōji}} 1). === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 9), "Nikabe no Kami" was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank Lower{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in Encho 5 (927), it is listed in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshiki District|Q8192583|WD=Q8192583|1=Yoshiki District, Yamaguchi}} as "Nikabe Jinja" and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (the only one in Yoshiki District){{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After that, divine rank rose in {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|WD=Q23422|1=939}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 2), {{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}} ({{ill|lt=Eiho|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1), {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|WD=Q19702|1=1180}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|WD=Q1194671|1=Jishō}} 4), and {{ill|lt=1187|Q19731|WD=Q19731|1=1187}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 3; equivalent to {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250|1=Genryaku}} 4), reaching Senior Third Rank{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In the "Suo Miyano-sho Tachi-kenmon" of {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745|1=1195}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 6), it appears as "Ninko-gu-sha," and shrine lands are recorded as 6 cho 20 bu of lower-upper-class fields, 3 cho 9 dan 20 bu of middle-middle-class fields, and 2 cho of upper-lower-class fields{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In Suo Province it was long ranked third after {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} (ichinomiya) and {{ill|lt=Izumo Shrine (Yamaguchi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (ninomiya). When {{ill|lt=Ouchi Yoshioki|Q909270|WD=Q909270|1=Ōuchi Yoshioki}} returned from campaign in {{ill|lt=Kyushu|Q13987|WD=Q13987|1=Kyushu}} in {{ill|lt=1494|Q6693|WD=Q6693|1=1494}} ({{ill|lt=Meio|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 6) and reported victory to five shrines in Suo, this shrine received his worship third{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. In {{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 12), during {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro no Ran|Q11432567|WD=Q11432567|1=Ouchi Teruhiro Rebellion}}, when {{ill|lt=Ouchi Teruhiro|Q11432566|WD=Q11432566|1=Ōuchi Teruhiro}} raided Yamaguchi, the shrine burned, but it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Mori Terumoto|Q1142657|WD=Q1142657|1=Mōri Terumoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), and was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5) by {{ill|lt=Mori Yoshimoto|Q11547584|WD=Q11547584|1=Mōri Yoshimoto}}{{Sfn|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}} (Heisei 9), fire (arson) destroyed shrine buildings that had stood since Kyoho 5. The honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} (Heisei 12), the haiden and heiden in {{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|WD=Q1988|1=2001}} (Heisei 13), and the kagura hall in {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}} (Heisei 22)[https://www.minato-yamaguchi.co.jp/yama/news/digest/2010/1107/14p.html "Completion of kagura hall at Nikabe Shrine in Yamaguchi; 100 residents attend completion ceremony"] (Yamaguchi Shimbun, Nov. 7, 2010).. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), February 16: from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nikabe no Kami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}), March 10: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fourth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as "Nika no Kami". === Precinct === File:Nikabe-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Nikabe-jinja, heiden.jpg|Heiden File:Nikabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Nikabe-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Cultural properties === === Yamaguchi City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - early Edo period, made in 1608 (Keicho 13), total length 55.7 cm. Designated March 2, 1982 (Showa 57). The honden had also been designated a Yamaguchi Prefecture tangible cultural property on November 11, 1977 (Showa 52), but was lost in the 1997 fire. === Access === * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Line|Q851226|WD=Q851226|1=Yamaguchi Line}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Yamaguchi Station|Q858463|WD=Q858463|1=Kami-Yamaguchi Station}} or {{ill|lt=Miyano Station|Q6884378|WD=Q6884378|1=Miyano Station}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|author=|year=1912|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensanjo|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/114}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 114-115. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=Nikabe Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 36 Place Names of Yamaguchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Nikabe Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Nikabe-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|500201|Nikabe Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nikabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi (city)]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
